doc.default: All mines form a double-headed snake. A snake is a width-1 four-connected path with no loops or crossings, only one branch
Each four-connected mine area has a unique size from 1 to N (N = board size - 1)
Sometimes you need total mine count to deduce
Each row has exactly one group of consecutive mines
Mine distribution in the first row equals the last row, and mine distribution in the first column equals the last column
All mines must be part of 1x1~1x4 rectangles or diagonals of length 2~4, these shapes cannot touch each other (not even diagonally).
For all non-mine cells, the mine count in their 8-neighbours is the same
Total mines on all parallel planes are equal
Each mine must have at least one dyed cell among its 4 adjacent cells, and dyed cells cannot be mines
The gray number on the board indicates that if that cell is a mine, it represents the number of mines around it.

The board is divided into n² equal square regions, each region is either all empty or all equal to an nxn pattern, where the pattern's mine positions correspond to non-empty regions and non-mine positions correspond to empty regions.

Each row has different mine counts; in each column, the highest mine's height is not lower than the highest mine in the left column (special case: if a column has no mines, all columns to its left have no mines)
For each interactive key, the total number of mines must satisfy the given comparison string.
Every mine must have at least one horizontally adjacent mine
A2 is a mine
Mines must form a triple in horizontal, vertical or diagonal directions
No two mines can be adjacent to each other
Mine region is 26-connected
(1) Dyed mines and undyed mines are each separately 8-connected; (2) At least one mine exists in each of the dyed and undyed regions
All mines form L-shapes: mines always form L-shapes with width 1 and one turn, L-shapes can be diagonally adjacent but not horizontally or vertically adjacent
Each row has different consecutive mine lengths
No mines exist in the UN, UE, NW, ES, DW, or DS positions relative to any mine (anti-knight pattern for hex grid)
Each mine counts as X mines (X is the sum of its row and column numbers)
The leftmost mine in each row counts as two mines
The mine arrangement in the 8 surrounding cells of each non-mine cell must be unique.
All 4-connected non-mine regions form solid squares
All four-connected mine/non-mine areas have size greater than 2
You can input multiple left rules separated by ':' to express AND relationship (board will follow both rule A and rule B)

For each (x,y), exactly one cell across all layers has a mine. If (x0,y0,z0) has a mine, the number of mines in {(x, y, z)| |x - x0| <= 1, |y - y0| <= 1, |z - z0| <= 1} equals its 2D neighbor count + 1
Echo: outputs the parameter
Starting from any mine cell, can traverse all mine cells without repetition or omission using knight moves
Exactly one way to select exactly one mine from each row and column
Sudoku Box: The board side length must be a multiple of 3. The board is evenly divided into 9 boxes, and numbers in each box must be unique.(V only)
Mines in dyed cells must have exactly 1 mine in their 6 adjacent cells
No mine is adjacent to another mine
Different four-connected mine areas cannot be diagonally adjacent
All mine cells form a Hamiltonian circuit via 8-connectivity
All mines form a snake. A snake is a width-1 orthogonal path without branches, loops or intersections
Non-mine cells must have at least one mine in the four orthogonal adjacent cells
Each mine area is a rectangle with width 1 and length no more than 4, rectangles cannot be diagonally adjacent
For a cell in row X or column X, the eight surrounding cells cannot have exactly X mines
Each row has a different number of mines
Sudoku: Each row and column of clue cells must contain unique numbers.(V only)
Square board of size 2n: each 2x2 block forms a region; the mine count in each region forms a Latin square (rows/cols have distinct values), and exactly n distinct values appear globally.
No mine is horizontally adjacent to another mine
All torus four-connected mine areas have an area of 3
Random rule idea: randomly select words and have LLM generate a rule description, then output it
Mine areas are connected via knight's moves (8-connectivity via knight's move pattern)
All mines form two 4-connected components, bottom-left and top-right corners must be mines and not connected

For every 2x2 sub-board, the arrangement of mines inside it is uniquely determined by the total number of mines in that sub-board; hence any two 2x2 areas with the same mine count must have identical mine patterns.

Mine distribution is randomly symmetric in one of the following ways: [horizontal/vertical/diagonal/anti-diagonal/center/Rotate 90 degrees] (arg: number, zero‑based index to choose fixed symmetry: [horizontal/vertical/diagonal/anti-diagonal/center/Rotate 90 degrees])
Mines cannot form a triple in horizontal, vertical or diagonal directions
All mines form a snake. A snake is a width-1 6-connected path with no branches, loops, or crossings
Dyed cells have mine value multiplied by 2 (total mine count unaffected)
Each 2x2 area cannot contain all four states (dyed mine, undyed mine, dyed non-mine, undyed non-mine) simultaneously
No four mines form the vertices of a square.
Each 2x2 area has at least one empty space
Uses sub-board to represent row segment boundaries: no boundary means same value, different sides must be boundary, boundary count increases from bottom up with row inheritance
Each group consists of exactly 1 non-square rectangle and 2 square rectangles; short sides diagonally touch the two square rectangles, groups do not touch each other
Dyed cells count as two mines, row and column 'multi-mine values' are equal. (Clue cells are not necessarily multi-mine rules)
Mines connect from any of the eight directions to outside the board
No straight boundary line of length 2 or more exists between mine and non-mine areas
Library with scrambled mine-values; solver must restore mapping
Each mine has exactly 1 or 3 mines in the four surrounding cells
1. All mines must be connected according to the auxiliary board's 4-direction connectivity; 2. Mines have only 3 connection methods (vertical/horizontal/diagonal/anti-diagonal)
Mines connect from any of the four directions to outside the board
The multiset of row mine counts equals the multiset of column mine counts.
No mine at knight's move distance from any mine
For each row, for every 4-length segment [a,b,c,d]: if b==c then d==b else d!=a.
Each 3x1 area determines whether the cell below its middle is a mine; evolution is shared across all areas of the current board
All mines must have at least one adjacent mine vertically
Mine value becomes negative in dyed cells
Every 2x2 area contains at least one mine
The orthogonally connected graph of non-mine cells is a tree.
(1) No three mines form a line horizontally or vertically; (2) No three non-mines form a line horizontally or vertically
In dyed cells, exactly one mine in the four surrounding cells
Mine value equals the sum of row and column numbers, but starting from 0
For each row, there is exactly one column that has the same mine count.
Each cell is randomly assigned a left rule. If the cell is a mine, the entire board must satisfy that cell's rule
(1) Equal mine count in each row; (2) Equal mine count in each column
Fetches a random quote from hitokoto.cn
The board has exactly three four-connected mine areas
The gray number on the board indicates that if that cell is a mine, it represents the number of mines around the corresponding cell on the opposite board.
Each row is divided into exactly three contiguous segments (Mine-NonMine-Mine or NonMine-Mine-NonMine), each segment at least one cell
Each column has the same number of mines
Top mine of a vertical consecutive group gets the group size, others get 0
Mine value equals the factorial of the number of mines in its 4 adjacent cells
Each row cannot simultaneously contain dyed non-mine, undyed non-mine, dyed mine, and undyed mine
Any four mines cannot form the vertices of a horizontally and vertically straight rectangle
Mine areas are 4-connected to the bottom edge of the board
The topmost mine in each column counts as two mines (does not affect the total mine count)
Mine value is 1 if there are more mines than non-mines in its row and column, otherwise -1
Each mine has exactly 2 or 3 mines in the eight surrounding cells
(1) Mines cannot form a triple in horizontal, vertical, or up-down directions; (2) Non-mines cannot form a triple in horizontal, vertical, or up-down directions.
Each row has the same number of mines
In each 4x4 block, the number of mines in dyed and non-dyed cells is equal
For all positions with the same digit, the mine pattern around them must be identical under rotation/reflection symmetry.
Divides the board into a quadtree; subtrees where all nodes are mines are called heavy-mines, other nodes cannot have two child nodes that are both mines or heavy-mines
Generates fixed 8-direction arrow diagram, mine cells must be traversed along arrows without missing or repeating
The snake has exactly 5 straight segments
In each row, mines are numbered from 1 left-to-right
Each row is obtained by deleting two consecutive cells from the previous row, shifting all cells to the right of the deleted cells two positions to the left, and then adding any two cells at the end.
Each row has one continuous group of mines, and each row has a different number of mines

Grid size 8 or 27. Partition into 2×2 (size 8) or 3×3 (size 27) blocks. Block value = sum of mines in the block. These values form an n×n matrix (n=4 or 9). In this matrix: each row, each column, and each sub-square of size block_size × block_size (aligned with the original blocks) contains distinct values. The total number of distinct block values occurring in the whole matrix must equal n.
The average position of all mines is an integer point
Sudoku Box+: The board side length must be a multiple of 3. The board is evenly divided into 9 boxes, and numbers in each row, column, and box must be unique.(V only)
Each mine has at least one mine in the four diagonally adjacent cells
Each mine is surrounded by an even number of mines in the eight adjacent cells (including 0)
All mines cannot be adjacent to other mines or diagonally adjacent
Any three mines can not form a triangle with an area of 0.5
All mines form a snake. A snake is a width-1 four-connected path with no branches, loops, or crossings. The top, bottom, left, and right of the board are considered connected.
Total mines in all 8 directions around a mine are equal
In each row, either there are no horizontally adjacent mines, or all have at least one horizontally adjacent mine.
For column N, total mines = |col(N-1) + col(N-2)| or |col(N-1) - col(N-2)|, cyclic indices.
Each 3x5 area forms a 0-F digit; sum of mines in inner and outer cells equals the digit value
A mine's left and down neighbors have different states.
All four-connected mine areas have an area of 3
All mines form a snake. A snake is a width-1 knight-connected path with no branches, loops, or crossings
All 4-connected mine blocks can be rearranged by independent translation to form an axis-aligned rectangle
(1) Each row has different mine counts; (2) Each column has different mine counts
Total number of mines is given
Requires that at least one row, one column, one main diagonal, or one anti-diagonal is completely filled with mines.

Every non-overlapping 2x2 block (stride 2) must be a corner of some 4x4 block. The 4x4 is split into four 2x2 subblocks. Each cell in the original 2x2 corresponds to the subblock at the same relative position. If that subblock contains 0 or 1 mines → the cell is empty; 3 or 4 mines → the cell is a mine; 2 mines → free.
A mine's left and down neighbors have the same state. (~ prefix: non-mine, ~ suffix: different) (No constraint for column A and last row)
Every odd-length (>=3) square contains an odd number of mines

Mine distribution is randomly symmetric-inversed in one of the following ways, except its own position: [horizontal/vertical/diagonal/anti-diagonal/center/Rotate 90 degrees] (arg: number, zero‑based index to choose fixed symmetry: [horizontal/vertical/diagonal/anti-diagonal/center/Rotate 90 degrees])
The parameter specifies the mine placement matrix. 0, 1, and X represent non-mine, mine, and uncertain respectively.
All orthogonally connected mine areas have area 4
Hex No-Square: Among any three mutually adjacent cells, at least one must be a mine
No mine-empty-mine pattern exists horizontally or vertically
All mine cells are on or inside an 8-connected mine ring
Some Dick: The minefield consists of several patterns of 4 cells (minimum) dick, which can intersect and share B cells, but cannot share A cells.
Non-mine areas are four-connected; each mine area is connected to the board boundary via four-connection.
A non-mine's left and down neighbors have the same state.
There is exactly one mine in the eight knight-move positions of the mine in the dyed cell
(1) All orthogonally connected mine areas are rectangles; (2) All mine areas are diagonally adjacent
Every square with side length a power of 2 (>=2) contains an even number of mines
Raw mine value
All mines form a snake. A knight snake is a knight-move connected path with no branches, loops, or crossings

1 snake + 2 square rectangles = exact full cover. One snake endpoint diagonally touches two different square corners, the other endpoint touches none. No other contact (overlap/ortho/diag) between snake and squares.
No three mines can be equally spaced
No cell may have mines on all four orthogonal neighbors.
All mines form several bridges. A bridge is a path from the left boundary to the right boundary with eight-connected (horizontal or diagonal), width 1, and length equal to the board.
No mine is vertically adjacent to another mine
All non-mine cells form 1x2x1, 2x1x1, or 1x1x2 3D rectangles, i.e., each non-mine cell has exactly one 6-connected neighbor (all holes!!!)
A non-mine's left and down neighbors have different states.
All mines form a tree root structure. The structure starts from one mine in the first row and extends downward, left-down, and right-down.
Each mine is surrounded by exactly two mines in the four orthogonal adjacent cells
This is a rule that would confuse a male girl
Exactly two mines around each mine in the eight surrounding cells
Each mine area is a 1x2 or 2x1 rectangle
(1) All four-connected mine areas are 1xn or nx1 rectangles; (2) All mine areas are diagonally adjacent
Each mine belongs to a unique triple group (horizontal/vertical/diagonal)
All non-mines form several snakes. A snake is a width-1 four-connected path with no branches, loops, or crossings.
All mines must be in a triple in horizontal, vertical, or diagonal direction
In dyed cells, non-mines are treated as mines, and mines as non-mines
If a cell is a mine, its horizontally symmetric cell is not a mine
All mines are numbered from left to right, top to bottom. Clue indicates the sum of mine indices in the eight surrounding cells
No three mines form a line in horizontal, vertical or diagonal directions
The difference in mine counts between adjacent rows or columns does not exceed 1
All four-connected mine areas form solid squares
All mines form 1x2x1, 2x1x1, or 1x1x2 3D rectangles
Equal number of mines in dyed and undyed cells in each row
All mines form a loop. A loop is a width-1 diagonal path without branches or intersections, and the loop connects head to tail
All mines form a snake. A snake is a width-1 four-connected path with no branches, loops, or crossings, and the head and tail are connected.
"What does it mean?"
All mines are orthogonally connected, all non-mines are orthogonally connected, no 2x2 block of mines or non-mines exists
You can input multiple left rules separated by ':' to express OR relationship (board will follow either rule A or rule B)
Mine areas are connected diagonally (8-connectivity)
(1) Each row has mine count not greater than the previous row (2) Each column has mine count not greater than the left column
Every 2x2x2 region must contain at least 2 mines
Mines clue indicates the total combo value in four directions; n consecutive mines in the same direction have combo values from 1 to n
Clue indicates this cell is a non-mine
Mines with the same number are 4-connected
Mines clue represents the number of mines in the central symmetric range of its mine region
Mine clue indicates the absolute difference between its vertical distance to the highest mine and to the lowest mine in its column
Pearl: SL simple loop, white pearl goes straight through with at least one turn in adjacent cells, black pearl turns through with no turns in adjacent cells
Mines clue value represents the perimeter of its mine region
In the direction the mine points to, there exist n mines (not including itself)
Clues have different rules, each clue's rule will be marked separately
Mines clue indicates the number of non-mine cells in the surrounding 8 cells
Mines clue indicates the number of mine triples containing it; mine triples can overlap partially

Letter X is the total number of mines in a certain ? cell in the 3x3 area centered on one of the two board positions. If that position on the corresponding board is marked X=N, then the cell must be a mine, and the ? cell position is shared across all clues
Clue shows the number of mines in the eight cells surrounding the mirror position reflected across the diagonal from top-left to bottom-right of the board, not including the cell itself
Clues are replaced by letters, each letter corresponds to two clues, and each clue corresponds to two letters
Mines are redstone wires, clues are solid blocks, clue shows total number of redstone cells connected to this cell
Clue shows the number of mines in a 2-cell cross range (up, down, left, right, front, back)
If letter X has N mines among 7 of its 8 adjacent cells, then the cell marked with X=N must be a mine. The positions of those 7 cells are shared across all clues on the board
Clue shows the area of its 4-connected non-mine region
Clue indicates the number of visible mines in the four orthogonal directions; empty cells block visibility
The clue value indicates the number of grid cell centers visible from the clue, with line of sight blocked by mines (including boundaries).
Clue shows the number of mines in all diagonal and orthogonal directions
Clue shows the number of mines in dyed and undyed cells within knight-move area (order is not fixed)
Clue shows the number of mines in a 1-cell cross range
Clue shows the sum of Manhattan distances to the nearest 2 mines

Clue shows the ASCII character represented by the 8 surrounding cells, starting from the right cell and rotating counter‑clockwise, treating mines as 1 and empty cells as 0. Non‑printable or non‑ASCII characters are displayed as the Unknown.
Liar + Deviation combination rule
Contains rules: [V], [2X], [2X'], [2D], [2P], [2M], [2A]. Use '2#:' to exclude 2A
Clue indicates the total number of mines in the eight directions
Clue shows the number of non-mine cells visible in all eight directions (including the cell itself); mines block the line of sight
Clue indicates the number of continuous mine groups in the 3x3 area
Clue indicates that the cell is a non-mine
The clue indicates the number of non-mine cells visible in the four cardinal directions after passing through the first mine encountered. Mines block line of sight.
Clue (a_p) indicates the lp norm distance to the nearest mine as a. (p=0,1,2,00)
Clue indicates the area of the largest mine-free rectangle containing this cell
If letter X has N mines in its 8 knight-move positions, then the cell with X=N must be a mine
Clue shows the number of mines in dyed and non-dyed cells in a cross pattern within radius 2 (order undefined)
Liar + Cross combination rule (dyed)
Liar + Liar (double liar, ±2) combination rule
Liar + Cross' combination rule (dyed)
Combines two right-side variants via multiplication.
Clue value equals n + number of mines around the n-th empty neighbor
Clue indicates the sum of areas of orthogonally adjacent mine groups
Clue shows the number of mines in two cells pointing in one direction, with an arrow indicating the direction
Contains rules: [1L], [1N], [1E'], [1M1N], [1N1X], [2M], [2X']. Clue values are only zero.
Clue indicates the number of directions with mines in the eight directions
Clue shows the total number of mines in the center 3x3 area of one of the two board positions; each clue is either 1 greater or 1 less than the true value
Clue value indicates the area of the triangle formed by the three nearest mines, decimal rounded to one decimal place.
Liar + Multiple + Negative combination rule

Clue indicates the number of mines in the cells at distance 1 from the clue in a hexagonal grid.
In a square grid, the clue indicates the number of mines in the surrounding cells: up, down, left, right; odd columns (B,D,F,...) additionally include upper left and upper right; even columns (A,C,E,...) additionally include lower left and lower right.
Clue shows the maximum difference between adjacent two directions among four directions.
For each cell, displayed +N/-N = (8-neighbor mines(cyclic indices.)) − (unfixed global standard value (range: [0-8])).
Clue shows the total number of mines in the knight's move area centered on one of the two board positions
Each row and column has exactly one error clue. Error clues have values 1 greater or 1 less than the true value
Clue value is the product of Manhattan distances to the two nearest mines
Clue (a_p) indicates the lp norm distance to the nearest mine as a. (p=0,1,2,00)
Clue has only one possible value
Liar + Partition combination rule
Clue cells show the number of mines belonging to them in the 8 adjacent cells, and each cell must belong to exactly one visible clue.
If clue X has N mines around it, then the cell with X=N on the other board must be a mine
Liar + Modulo combination rule
Clue indicates the number of mines in surrounding eight cells; if there is a mine in a dyed cell, it counts as one mine (total mine count unaffected)
Each number indicates the number of mines in the eight surrounding cells. Two mines in opposite positions are counted as one mine.
Mines are redstone wires, clues are solid blocks, clue shows number of redstone connections on edges

The clue represent the number of switches activated within a 3x3 area of the board, after switches activated all board is left empty finally. (The clue also show whether the switch at this clue location is activated.)
Clue shows the number of mines in the surrounding 8 cells. All numbers shown on the board are already given. Other cells cannot have any of the shown numbers.
All clues are either 1 greater or 1 less than the true value

Includes the following rules: [1L], [1L1M], [1L1L], [1L1N], [1L1W], [1L1N], [1L1X], [1L1P], [1L1E]
Use [1L1#:] to exclude [1L1W]
Liar + Eyesight combination rule
Clue shows the number of mines in the surrounding cells: LL;LU;LD;L;RR;RU;RD;RR

Includes the following rules: [1L], [1L1M], [1L1L], [1L1N], [1L1W], [1L1N], [1L1X], [1L1P], [1L1E], [1L1X'], [1L1K], [1L1W'], [1L1E'], [1LMN], [1LMX], [1LNX]
Use [1L1#':] to exclude [1L1W]
Clue shows the number of directions (out of 8) where the nearest 2 cells contain a mine

Clues are replaced by letters, each letter corresponds to a clue, and each clue corresponds to a letter
Specify the rules used and their order through 2E#:<rule1>;<rule2>;...
Default is the same as the second generation
You can add 1# 1#' 2# 2#' 2#':

Clue shows the number of mines: 8 adjacent cells plus left-left and right-right; odd rows additionally include top-left-top and top-right-top; even rows additionally include bottom-left-bottom and bottom-right-bottom
Clue shows the number of mines in dyed or undyed cells within the 3x3 area
Clue indicates the number of mines in a cross pattern with radius 2
Clue shows the total mines in distance 1 and distance 2√2 regions
Clue indicates the length of each continuous mine group in the 3x3 area
Each row and column has exactly one mine counted as two
Clue shows the number of non-mine cells visible in the four diagonal directions (including the cell itself); mines block the line of sight
Clue shows the maximum number of mines among the four 3x3 areas centered at cells offset in the four directions
Liar + Knight combination rule
If letter X has N mines among ? of its 8 adjacent cells, then the cell with X=N must be a mine. The positions of those ? cells are shared across all clues on the board
Clue (a_p) indicates the lp norm distance to the nearest mine as a. (p=0,1,2,00)
Each row and column has exactly one non-liar clue. Liar clues have values 1 greater or less than the true value
Clues are replaced by letters, each letter corresponds to one clue, and each clue corresponds to one letter

If the four adjacent cells (up, down, left, right) contain an odd number of mines, the clue value is the total number of mines in the surrounding eight cells plus or minus 1 (but cannot be -1 or 9). If even, the clue value is the total number of mines in the surrounding eight cells.
Liar + Multiple (dyed mines count double) combination rule
Clue (a_p) indicates the lp norm distance to the nearest mine as a. (p=0,1,2,00)
Clue shows the number of mines within a distance of 2 from the cell
Clue shows the shortest continuous path length that connects all mines in the surrounding eight cells
Clue shows two hex digits representing mine pattern in 8 neighbors clockwise
If letter X has N mines in its 3x3 area, then the cell with X=N must be a mine
No mine is 0, has mine is 1, clue represents the bitwise XOR of two binary numbers from the corresponding row (left to right) and column (top to bottom)
Number shows the number of mines in 7 of the 8 surrounding cells. The positions of those 7 cells are shared across all clues on the board
The clue indicates that the area within its rectangular grid expands in four directions until it encounters a mine within the mine's range.
Clue indicates the number of visible cells in the opposite direction of each adjacent mine; mines block sight.
Clue indicates the total mine value at the eight knight's move positions
Clue shows the number of mines in the kinsho area (the six cells: up, down, left, right, up-left, up-right)
Clue indicates the number of mines in the eight surrounding cells that have no mines in their four orthogonal neighbors
Clue shows the number of 2x2 areas containing both the cell and at least one mine
Clue shows the maximum total mines among the four 2x2 areas containing the cell (only cells inside board are counted for edge areas)
Clue shows the number of mines in the surrounding cells: left and right; odd-numbered columns additionally include top; even-numbered columns additionally include bottom
Clue shows the total number of mines in the center 3x3 area of one of the two board positions
Clue shows the total number of mines in a 3x3 area centered one cell above
The clue indicates that the visible area outside of Ray (including itself) will be obstructed by Ray.
Each number indicates the sum of mine values in the surrounding eight cells
Each mine directly below another mine counts as two (total mine count is unlimited)
Clue shows the variance of vertical and horizontal sight multiplied by 2 and then take the square root. Or in other words, clue shows the difference between vertical and horizontal sight.
Clues have different rules, each clue's rule will be marked separately
Contains rules: [V], [1M], [1L], [1W], [1N], [1X], [1P], [1E]
Liar + Multiple + Cross combination rule
Clue shows the total number of mines in a 3x3 area centered one cell offset in any of the four directions (globally consistent)
Liar + Eyesight' combination rule
Clue shows the number of mines in dyed and undyed cells within the 3x3 area (order is not fixed)
Clue shows the number of mines in an Nx3 area, where N is the number of consecutive non-mine cells directly above
Liar + Longest Wall combination rule
Each mine's value equals the total mine count in its eight surrounding cells (including itself); total mine count is unaffected
Average of clue values for orthogonally connected non-mine cells
Each clue's multi-mine positions are fixed relative to the clue and shared across the board
Board has multiple master boards with identical mine positions but different rules; rules are labeled in sequence

Number shows how many steps the Langton's Ant moves from the clue cell until exiting the board.
Arrow shows the initial direction of the Langton's Ant; rotates clockwise 90° (right turn) when moving over non-mine cells, and counterclockwise 90° (left turn) when moving over mines.
The number can be infinite (shown as 0), indicating the path forms a loop
Each row and column has exactly one error clue. The value of the error clue cannot be the true value.
Clue (a_p) indicates the lp norm distance to the nearest mine as a. (p=0,1,2,00)
Each number indicates the number of lines connecting any two mines that pass through this cell, including boundaries and corners.
Inject R* as a left-line rule according to the parity of the total mine count
Clue shows the number of the four opposite cell pairs where exactly one cell is a mine (cells outside the board are treated as non-mines)
Two main boards exist, cells at the same position on both boards cannot both be mines. DJ:A+B;C+D means left board uses rules A and B, right board uses rules C and D
XOR the results of Langton's Ant [3A] in four directions; if the result is infinite, display using complement form
Clue shows the total number of mines in the center 3x3 area of two of the three board positions
Each number indicates the number of mines in the surrounding 26 cells
Clue indicates the number of horizontal, vertical, and diagonal lines of three in the surrounding 3x3 area that are all mines or all non-mines
Liar + Cross combination rule
Liar + Mini Cross combination rule
For clues other than 0 and 1, displays the largest prime factor of the true value
Clue shows the remainder when the number of surrounding mines is divided by 3
Each number indicates the number of mines in the surrounding 26 cells
Clue shows the number of non-mine cells visible when looking outward from the current cell in four directions, then rotating clockwise (similar to the swastika pattern); mines block the line of sight
Clue shows the number of adjacent mine regions in the four directions
Clue shows the maximum area of the four adjacent mine regions in the four directions
Each number indicates the number of mines in the surrounding eight cells
Each number indicates the number of mines in the surrounding eight cells
Clue shows the total number of mines at distance √2 and distance 2
Clue shows the number of ways to go from this cell to the bottom-right corner, moving only right or down
Each number indicates the sum of Chebyshev distances to the two nearest mines
Liar + Multiple + Negative + Cross combination rule
Clue has the same remainder as the number of mines in the 8 adjacent cells divided by 2
Clue indicates the sum of rows and columns in the 3x3 area that have exactly one mine
Clue is a randomly generated number
Liar + Negative (signed mine count) combination rule

Includes the following rules: [1L], [1L2X], [1L2D], [1L2M], [1L2A]
Use [1L2#:] to exclude [1L2A]
Clue indicates the number of consecutive non-mines perpendicular to the arrow direction from the clue to the mine or board edge
Clue shows the number of non-mine cells visible in the four orthogonal directions (including the cell itself); mines block the line of sight
Liar + Wall combination rule
If a mine has four or more mines in its eight surrounding cells, then that mine's value is 0; total mine count is unaffected
Clue shows the number of mines in all diagonal cells

Clue indicates the product of distances to the two nearest mines
Clue shows the number of non-mine cells visible in the knight's move directions (including the cell itself); mines block the line of sight

Clue is displayed as arrows (up/down/left/right) instead of numbers, pointing to the nearest mine in each direction. Multiple arrows are shown when multiple mines are at equal distance. Zero is displayed when there are no mines in any direction
Clue indicates, for each of the four orthogonal directions, the number of cells in the adjacent mine group that are farther than the clue in that direction
Clue shows the longest continuous mine length in a 3x3 range
Clue shows the difference in mine count between dyed and undyed cells in the 3x3 area of one of the two board positions
Error clue is 1 more or less than the true value; if the clue is in a 2x2 non-mine box, it's an error clue, otherwise it's the true value

Includes the following rules: [1L], [1L2X], [1L2D], [1L2M], [1L2X']
Use [1L2#':] to exclude [1L2A]
All clues are either 2 greater, 2 less than, or equal to the true value
Clue shows the number of mines in a 1-cell cross range (up, down, left, right, front, back)
Each number shows the absolute value of the sum of mine values in the surrounding eight cells
Liar + Area combination rule
Clue shows the number of mines in the upper 5 cells and lower 5 cells of the 8 surrounding cells (order not fixed)

Different rules for each row and column, shown at the top-left boundary. Left rules only affect their row and adjacent rows. (1B affects only row balance) Add parameters after : ? means random order, ! means random rules, then add specified rules separated by ; can specify only left or right rules. Empty : is equivalent to :!.
Clue shows the difference between vertical and horizontal eyesight, with an arrow indicating the direction with longer sight
The number indicates the mine count among N of the 8 adjacent cells. The positions of those N cells are shared across all clues on the board
Mines are light bulbs, and each bulb increases the brightness of any cell on the board by the inverse of the square of the distance. Clues indicate the total brightness of that cell.
Liar + Negative + Cross combination rule
Clue shows the number of mines in all cells in the same row or column

A clue cell contains several numbers, each corresponding to a row, indicating the number of mines in the four-connected region of the clue cell located in that row. The order of these numbers is uncertain.
